meta.title: Turginbay Bekzat | Django & Flutter Portfolio
meta.description: Portfolio of Turginbay Bekzat, a 2nd-year IT student at Satbayev University building web and mobile projects with Django and Flutter.
meta.ogDescription: Web and mobile projects by Turginbay Bekzat, including NaqUsta, QazSound, and a Telegram bot.

hero.title: I build web and mobile projects with Django and Flutter.
hero.body: I’m Bekzat from Kazakhstan. Right now I’m focused on full-stack web development with Django and mobile apps with Flutter, and I use personal projects to understand how UI, backend logic, and product structure fit together.
hero.statusValue: I’m currently exploring Clean Architecture, UI/UX design, and scalable backend systems.
hero.statusNote: My long-term goal is to become a world-class senior full-stack developer.
hero.signatureBody: Everything here comes from projects I actually built and documented over time.

about.title: I’m still early in the process, but I like building complete things.
about.lead: I study Information Technology at Satbayev University and use personal projects to get better at both web and mobile development.
about.introBody: My main path right now is full-stack web development with Django and mobile apps with Flutter.
about.philosophyBody: At the moment I’m spending a lot of time on Clean Architecture, UI/UX design, and scalable backend systems.
about.quote: I like bridging the gap between clean user interfaces and solid databases.

identity.label: Base
identity.note: That’s where I study and build from.

identity.label: Direction
identity.note: Web and mobile are the two areas I’m focused on most.

identity.label: Approach
identity.note: Real projects teach me more than isolated exercises.

skills.title: The tools I use most right now.
skills.lead: Most of my work sits between Django on the web side and Flutter on the mobile side, with the usual frontend and tooling around them.
skills.labTitle: What I’m trying to get better at
skills.labBody: Alongside the main stack, I’m actively learning the parts that make projects cleaner and easier to grow.

groups.label: 01 / Backend
groups.title: Python and Django
groups.description: This is the main web backend path I’m building on right now.

groups.label: 02 / Mobile
groups.title: Flutter and Firebase
groups.description: I use Flutter for mobile apps and Firebase for auth, data, and storage when the project needs it.

groups.label: 03 / Frontend
groups.title: HTML, CSS, JavaScript
groups.description: For web projects, I still like understanding the basics directly instead of hiding them behind a big framework.

groups.label: 04 / Tools
groups.title: Daily tools
groups.description: These are the tools I use constantly while building and studying.

projectsSection.title: Three projects that show where I am right now.
projectsSection.lead: They’re different in scope, but each one helped me learn something concrete about product structure, backend logic, or workflow.

journey.title: Where I am, what I’m learning, and where I want to go.
journey.lead: I don’t think of this as a fixed plan. It’s more a clear direction based on what I’m studying and building now.
journey.closingBody: I want to keep building until web, mobile, UI, and backend work all feel natural in the same project.

phases.title: 2nd-year IT student at Satbayev University
phases.body: My foundation right now is university study plus a lot of self-driven project work outside class.

phases.title: Full-stack web and mobile development
phases.body: My main direction is Django for the web side and Flutter for mobile apps.

phases.title: Clean Architecture, UI/UX, scalable backends
phases.body: These are the areas I’m spending the most time on right now because they change how solid a project feels.

phases.title: Become a world-class senior full-stack developer
phases.body: That’s the direction I’m working toward, step by step, through real projects and better engineering habits.

contact.title: If you want to reach me, these are the links I actually use.
